snapshot.readinessLevel: Low — recovering from today's long run

continue: Today's long run is the model — 8 mi at avg HR 128, genuinely easy Zone 2. THIS is exactly what builds the aerobic base for a sub-3 marathon. Do more of precisely this and your easy % will keep climbing.
Load settled back to optimal — ACWR 1.2 after Thursday's interval spike. You recovered the hard session well; the engine is handling the buildup.
Last 7 days hit ~30 mi — a recent high and right on the trajectory a sub-3 build needs. Keep stacking easy volume.
HRV is steady at 55 ms (balanced) and resting HR low (48) — recovery markers stay green, so it's safe to keep progressing ~10%/week.
change: Polarized split is improving — easy miles rose to 28% (from 20%) thanks to today's run, but 50% is still gray (avg HR ~139–151). For a sub-3 build you want ~80% easy / ~20% hard. Keep forcing easy days to HR <135; it's the highest-leverage habit between now and Oct 2027.

Build the aerobic base — the #1 driver toward sub-3. You're averaging ~14 mi/wk (30 in the last 7); the multi-month plan is to ramp consistently toward 40–50 mi/wk, almost all easy. Garmin still flags “Aerobic Low Shortage.”
VO2max sits at 52.5; sub-3 generally needs it climbing toward the upper 50s. The weekly VO2/threshold session plus rising easy volume is what moves it — be patient, it's a 4–6 week-per-step metric.
Weight is trending down — 173.2 lb (BMI 24.2), -1.2 from your first log. Getting toward your low-160s also helps marathon pace (less to carry). Hold the ~1 lb/week pace.
Sleep dipped last night (73). Keep a consistent 7.5 h+ bedtime — recovery is what lets you absorb rising mileage without breaking down.
